meta_title: Impact Doors in Boulevard Gardens, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Boulevard Gardens, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind-rated, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors for Boulevard Gardens Homes Built to Handle South Florida's Worst
intro:
Boulevard Gardens is a tight-knit Broward County community where longtime residents take pride in their homes and their neighbors. With average home values around $250,000 and a neighborhood where roughly 65% of housing stock was built before 1994, many properties here were constructed well before Florida adopted the rigorous High Velocity Hurricane Zone building standards that now govern new construction and major renovations. That gap between older construction and modern storm protection is exactly why so many Boulevard Gardens homeowners are making the switch to impact-rated doors — and why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has become a trusted name throughout the ZIP code 33311 area.

Sitting approximately four miles from the coast, Boulevard Gardens may not face the immediate shoreline surge risks of beachfront communities, but make no mistake: the community sits squarely in Broward County's 170mph wind zone and within the HVHZ designation, which means hurricane-force winds, flying debris, and driving rain are real, recurring threats. When storms like Hurricane Irma (2017) or Hurricane Wilma (2005) sweep across Broward County, inland communities like Boulevard Gardens experience the same destructive wind fields and pressure differentials that shatter unprotected doors and compromise home envelopes. Standard entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors simply are not built to survive these conditions — impact-rated doors are.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand that homeowners in Boulevard Gardens are making careful, budget-conscious decisions about protecting the investment they've built over years. That's why we pair world-class products from ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI with transparent pricing, flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, and 100% in-house installation from a team with over 60 years of combined experience serving Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Protecting your front door, back patio sliding door, or elegant French doors isn't a luxury — in a neighborhood like Boulevard Gardens, it's one of the smartest home improvements you can make.

section_why.h2: Why Boulevard Gardens Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
When it comes to impact door installation in Boulevard Gardens, there's a meaningful difference between a company that operates out of a call center and one that has physically worked on hundreds of homes just like yours across Broward County. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed work on over 500 homes throughout the tricounty area, and our crews are intimately familiar with the construction styles, permit processes, and specific site challenges common to communities like Boulevard Gardens — from older concrete block homes with non-standard door framing to CBS construction that requires specialized installation techniques to maintain structural integrity during high-wind events.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from many competitors is our commitment to 100% in-house installation. We don't subcontract your project to third-party laborers who may have variable experience with HVHZ compliance requirements. Every technician on your job site is a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee, trained directly by our team, and accountable to our company's A+ BBB rating and the standards we've spent decades building. This matters especially in Boulevard Gardens, where a significant portion of homes require careful assessment of existing door frames, threshold conditions, and rough opening dimensions before any new impact door system can be properly engineered and installed.

Our product portfolio for impact doors centers on two industry-leading brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both lines offer HVHZ-approved impact door systems with multi-point locking mechanisms, heavy-duty hardware, and impact-laminated glass that meets or exceeds Broward County's strict permitting requirements. Whether you're replacing a worn entry door, upgrading a sliding glass door that leads to a back patio, or installing French doors in a living area, our team will help you select the right product for your home's specific exposure, your design preferences, and your budget.

Financing is available starting at $87 per month with $0 down options, making it possible for Boulevard Gardens families to protect their homes without waiting years to save the full cost upfront. We're licensed, fully insured, and ready to guide you through every step — from the free in-home assessment through final inspection with the Broward County Building Division.

section_history.h2: Boulevard Gardens Has Faced the Storm — Here's What History Teaches Us
section_history.body:
Since 1990, the South Florida region that includes Boulevard Gardens has been impacted by no fewer than 10 named storms, each leaving behind lessons about structural vulnerability and the true cost of under-protected homes. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains the benchmark event that rewrote the book on Florida building codes. While Andrew's epicenter struck Miami-Dade County with catastrophic force, the storm's wind field extended well into Broward County, damaging homes in communities throughout the area and exposing a generation of doors, windows, and roofing systems as dangerously inadequate. Many of the homes in Boulevard Gardens that predate 1994 were built under the very code standards Andrew destroyed — a sobering reality for current owners.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of threat to Broward County communities. Wilma tracked across the entire peninsula, and its pressure gradient produced some of the most powerful sustained winds Broward County had experienced in decades. Countless front entry doors buckled, sliding glass doors were sucked out of their tracks, and French door assemblies failed along their center seams under the vacuum pressures Wilma generated. Communities just like Boulevard Gardens, where older door systems were common, saw disproportionate damage compared to homes with modern impact-rated assemblies.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a stark reminder that even storms that don't make direct landfall nearby can devastate inland communities. Irma's wind field was broad enough to lash Broward County with powerful gusts that tested every door and window in the region. For Boulevard Gardens homeowners who had already upgraded to impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, Irma was a non-event. For those who hadn't, the storm brought broken glass, breached entries, and costly interior water damage. The lesson from Boulevard Gardens' storm history is consistent: impact doors are not a precaution — they are a necessity.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and HVHZ Standards: What Boulevard Gardens Homeowners Must Know
section_code.body:
Boulevard Gardens falls within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) designation, which places it under the most stringent building code requirements in the United States. The HVHZ was formally established following the catastrophic failures exposed by Hurricane Andrew in 1992, and it covers all of Miami-Dade and Broward counties. This designation means that any impact door installed in Boulevard Gardens must meet the specific product approval standards outlined in the Florida Building Code, Chapter 14, and must carry either a Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or a Florida Product Approval — or both. Not every impact door sold in Florida qualifies for installation in the HVHZ, which is why working with a knowledgeable installer like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is critical to ensuring your project passes inspection.

In practical terms, HVHZ compliance for impact doors in Boulevard Gardens means the products must be tested and rated to withstand large-missile impact testing, cyclic pressure loading, and the specific wind pressure requirements associated with the area's 170mph design wind speed. Multi-point locking systems are not just a premium feature in this context — they are often a code requirement for door assemblies of certain widths and configurations. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's ESW and CWI door systems are fully vetted for HVHZ installation and come with the documentation your inspector will require.

All impact door replacements in Boulevard Gardens require permits issued through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the permitting process on your behalf, preparing and submitting all required documentation, product approval numbers, and installation specifications. After installation, a Broward County inspector will visit the property to verify code compliance before the permit is closed. This process protects you as a homeowner and ensures your investment is legally recognized — which matters both for insurance purposes and for future property resale.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Engineered for Boulevard Gardens
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact door systems from two brands that consistently earn top marks for performance, durability, and HVHZ compliance: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These are not big-box store products or catalog brands — they are professional-grade impact door systems designed specifically for South Florida's demanding climate and strict code environment. Both product lines are available in configurations suited to the most common door needs in Boulevard Gardens homes, including impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French door systems.

ESW impact doors are engineered with heavy-gauge aluminum frames, impact-laminated glass panels, and multi-point locking hardware that secures the door at multiple points along the frame rather than at a single latch point. This multi-point system dramatically increases the door's resistance to both positive and negative wind pressure — the kind of inward and outward forces that cause standard doors to fail during hurricane conditions. ESW entry doors are available in a range of panel styles and finish options, allowing Boulevard Gardens homeowners to upgrade their storm protection without sacrificing curb appeal. Their sliding glass door and French door options bring the same structural integrity to larger openings that are especially vulnerable during wind events.

CWI impact door systems offer a complementary set of options with a focus on design flexibility and long-term performance in humid, high-UV coastal and near-coastal environments. CWI's weather-resistant frame construction resists salt air corrosion and thermal expansion cycling, making these doors well-suited for the South Florida climate even at Boulevard Gardens' inland location. Both ESW and CWI products carry the product approvals required for HVHZ installation and are available with Low-E glass options that can improve your home's energy efficiency alongside its storm protection — a dual benefit that resonates strongly with budget-conscious homeowners throughout the 33311 ZIP code.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Boulevard Gardens — and Why the Investment Pays Off
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Boulevard Gardens, where the average home value sits around $250,000, the decision to invest in impact doors is one that deserves a clear-eyed look at both cost and return. Impact door installation costs vary depending on the type of door (entry, sliding glass, or French door), the size of the opening, and the specific product selected. In the current South Florida market, a single impact-rated entry door installation typically ranges from approximately $1,500 to $3,500 fully installed, while sliding glass door systems or French door configurations may range from $2,500 to $6,000 or more depending on size and complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, no-obligation in-home estimates so Boulevard Gardens homeowners know exactly what their specific project will cost before any commitment is made.

For homeowners who don't want to pay the full project cost upfront, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options designed with Broward County families in mind. Qualified buyers can get started with $0 down and monthly payments from $87 per month, spreading the investment over manageable terms without sacrificing protection while you save. This makes impact door upgrades accessible even for lower-middle income households throughout Boulevard Gardens who might otherwise delay a critical safety improvement.

The return on this investment extends well beyond storm protection. Impact doors add measurable value to Boulevard Gardens homes, with many real estate professionals noting that HVHZ-compliant impact openings are a selling point that resonates strongly with informed buyers in Broward County. Additionally, the energy savings from properly sealed, insulated impact door systems can reduce monthly cooling costs year-round — a meaningful benefit in South Florida's extended summer season. When you factor in insurance premium discounts (which we'll detail in the next section), the total financial picture for impact doors in Boulevard Gardens becomes even more compelling.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Can Reduce Your Homeowner's Insurance Costs in Boulevard Gardens
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County has become one of the most significant ongoing expenses for Florida property owners, and Boulevard Gardens residents are no exception. Premiums for properties in the HVHZ without storm-rated openings are substantially higher than for comparable homes that have compliant impact windows and doors. When you install impact doors through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you become eligible to request a wind mitigation inspection — a professional assessment that documents your home's storm-resistant features for submission to your insurance carrier. This report can unlock meaningful premium discounts that persist year after year for as long as you own the home.

Under Florida's wind mitigation credit system, homeowners who can demonstrate that all exterior openings — including doors — are protected with HVHZ-approved impact products may qualify for credits that reduce their windstorm premium by a substantial margin. The exact discount varies by insurer and coverage structure, but many Boulevard Gardens homeowners report annual savings ranging from several hundred dollars to well over a thousand dollars per year after a full impact door and window upgrade. Major carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance and numerous private market insurers, all recognize wind mitigation credits as defined by the Florida Department of Financial Services.

Beyond premium discounts, impact doors may also reduce your deductible exposure in the event of a claim. Many Florida homeowner's policies include a separate hurricane deductible — often 2% to 5% of the insured value — that applies whenever a named storm causes damage. Homes with fully protected openings are far less likely to sustain interior water damage or structural breaches during a storm, which means you're less likely to face that deductible at all. For a Boulevard Gardens home valued at $250,000, even a 2% hurricane deductible represents a $5,000 out-of-pocket exposure that a properly installed impact door system helps you avoid.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Boulevard Gardens if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Boulevard Gardens sits within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) and is rated for 170mph wind speeds, regardless of its inland location. Wind damage is not limited to coastal areas — the same rotating wind fields that affect beachfront homes reach every neighborhood in Broward County. Standard doors can fail at wind speeds well below what South Florida storms regularly produce. HVHZ-rated impact doors are the code-compliant and safest choice for your home.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Boulevard Gardens, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Boulevard Gardens typically range from approximately $1,500 to $3,500 for a single entry door installation and $2,500 to $6,000 or more for sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified buyers. We provide free in-home estimates so you'll know the exact cost for your specific project before making any commitment.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door replacements in Boulevard Gardens require permits issued by the Broward County Building Division. The permits ensure that installed products meet HVHZ building code requirements and that installation is performed correctly. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the full permitting process on your behalf — we prepare and submit all documentation, product approvals, and installation specifications, and we coordinate the final inspection with the county so you don't have to manage the paperwork.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a Boulevard Gardens home?
faqs.a: Most impact door installations in Boulevard Gardens can be completed in one to two days, depending on the number of openings and the scope of the project. The permitting process with the Broward County Building Division typically takes one to three weeks before installation can begin, depending on current permit volume. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will walk you through the expected timeline during your free in-home estimate, including scheduling the post-installation inspection required to close the permit.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Boulevard Gardens?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Once your impact doors are installed and permitted, you can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector. The resulting report documents your HVHZ-compliant openings and can be submitted to your insurance carrier for windstorm premium credits. Many Broward County homeowners report significant annual savings after upgrading to impact doors and windows. The exact discount depends on your insurer and current policy structure, but the long-term savings often meaningfully offset the cost of the upgrade.
